## Visual Basic

Public Class ConfigItemAttribute

Inherits [System.Attribute](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.attribute)

## C#

public class ConfigItemAttribute **: [Attribute](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.attribute)**

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

**Namespace:**
GTA

**Assembly:** ScriptHookDotNet (in ScriptHookDotNet.dll)

| Metadata (inherited from **HandleObject**) | Allows you to store Metadata on an object. Metadata can be read back as long as the object exists. Usage: object.Metadata.MyValueName = MyData |

| NavigationStyle | Not sure what it does or which values it takes. Common values are 0, 1 and 2. |
| RetreatingBehavior | Not sure what it does or which values it takes. Common values are 1 and 2. |
| SightRange | Range of sight in meters/yards. |
| StandingStyle | Not sure what it does or which values it takes. |
| TargetInjuredReaction | Not sure what it does or which values it takes. Common value is 0. |

   [System.ValueType](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.valuetype)
      **GTA.Matrix**

## Visual Basic

Public Structure Matrix

Implements [System.IEquatable(Of GTA.Matrix)](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.iequatable(of gta.matrix))

## C#

public struct Matrix **: [System.IEquatable<GTA.Matrix>](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.iequatable-1)**

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

**Namespace:**
GTA

**Assembly:** ScriptHookDotNet (in ScriptHookDotNet.dll)

<a id="gta-matrixmembers"></a>

## Matrix Members

Matrix overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Identity | Gets a Matrix that represents an identity matrix. |

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Determines the sum of two matrices. |
| AffineTransformation | Creates a 3D affine transformation matrix. |
| AffineTransformation2D | Creates a 2D affine transformation matrix. |
| Billboard | Creates a spherical billboard that rotates around a specified object position. |
| Divide | Overloaded. Scales a matrix by the given value. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal. |
| Invert | Overloaded. Calculates the inverse of the specified matrix. |
| Lerp | Performs a linear interpolation between two matricies. |
| LookAtLH | Creates a left-handed, look-at matrix. |
| LookAtRH | Creates a right-handed, look-at matrix. |
| Multiply | Overloaded. Scales a matrix by the given value. |
| Negate | Negates a matrix. |
| OrthoLH | Creates a left-handed, orthographic projection matrix. |
| OrthoRH | Creates a right-handed, orthographic projection matrix. |
| PerspectiveLH | Creates a left-handed, perspective projection matrix. |
| PerspectiveRH | Creates a right-handed, perspective projection matrix. |
| RotationAxis | Creates a matrix that rotates around an arbitary axis. |
| RotationQuaternion | Creates a rotation matrix from a quaternion. |
| RotationX | Creates a matrix that rotates around the x-axis. |
| RotationY | Creates a matrix that rotates around the y-axis. |
| RotationYawPitchRoll | Creates a rotation matrix with a specified yaw, pitch, and roll. |
| RotationZ | Creates a matrix that rotates around the z-axis. |
| Scaling | Overloaded. Creates a matrix that scales along the x-axis, y-axis, and y-axis. |
| Subtract | Determines the difference between two matrices. |
| Transformation | Creates a transformation matrix. |
| Transformation2D | Creates a 2D transformation matrix. |
| Translation | Overloaded. Creates a translation matrix using the specified offsets. |
| Transpose | Calculates the transpose of the specified matrix. |

#### Public Static (Shared) Operators

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Addition Operator | Adds two matricies. |
| Division Operator | Overloaded. Divides two matricies. |
| Equality Operator | Tests for equality between two objects. |
| Inequality Operator | Tests for inequality between two objects. |
| Multiplication Operator | Overloaded. Multiplies two matricies. |
| Subtraction Operator | Subtracts two matricies. |
| Unary Negation Operator | Negates a matrix. |

Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal.

Overloads Public Shared Function Equals( \_
   ByRef *value1* As GTA.Matrix, \_
   ByRef *value2* As GTA.Matrix \_
) As [Boolean](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.boolean)

## C#

public static [bool](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.boolean) Equals(
   ref GTA.Matrix *value1*,
   ref GTA.Matrix *value2*
);

#### Parameters

*value1*

*value2*

#### Return Value

`true` if *value1* is the same instance as *value2* or if both are `null` references or if `value1.Equals(value2)` returns `true`; otherwise, `false`.

<a id="gta-matrix-lerp"></a>

## Lerp Method

Performs a linear interpolation between two matricies.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Function Lerp( \_
   ByVal *start* As GTA.Matrix, \_
   ByVal *end* As GTA.Matrix, \_
   ByVal *amount* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
) As GTA.Matrix

## C#

public static GTA.Matrix Lerp(
   GTA.Matrix *start*,
   GTA.Matrix *end*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *amount*
);

#### Parameters

*start*
:   Start matrix.

*end*
:   End matrix.

*amount*
:   Value between 0 and 1 indicating the weight of *end*.

#### Return Value

The linear interpolation of the two matrices.

#### Remarks

This method performs the linear interpolation based on the following formula.

```
start + (end - start) * amount
```

Passing *amount* a value of 0 will cause *start* to be returned; a value of 1 will cause *end* to be returned.

#### See Also

Matrix Class | GTA Namespace

---

safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

| DuckWhenAimedAtByGroupMember |  |

<a id="gta-pedmethods"></a>

## Methods

The methods of the **Ped** class are listed below. For a complete list of **Ped** class members, see the Ped Members topic.

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| ApplyForce | Overloaded. Applies a force given in world coordinates to the ped. |
| ApplyForceRelative | Overloaded. Applies a force that is relative to the current orientation of the ped. Directions: positive X is right, positive Y is front, positive Z is Top |
| AttachBlip |  |
| AttachTo | Attaches the ped to a vehicle WITHOUT physics! |
| BecomeMissionCharacter |  |
| CancelAmbientSpeech |  |
| CantBeDamagedByRelationshipGroup |  |
| ChangeRelationship |  |
| Delete |  |
| Detach |  |
| Die |  |
| DropCurrentWeapon |  |
| Equals (inherited from **HandleObject**) | Overloaded. |
| Exists (inherited from **object**) | Returns whether the object still exists in game. Other calls to non-existing objects will result in an error. This function is FAST and is better checked too much rather than too less. |
| FleeByVehicle |  |
| ForceHelmet |  |
| ForceRagdoll |  |
| GetBonePosition |  |
| GetControllingPlayer |  |
| GetHashCode (inherited from **HandleObject**) |  |
| GetOffsetPosition | Returns the given offset in world coordinates. Directions: positive X is right, positive Y is front, positive Z is Top |
| GiveFakeNetworkName |  |
| HasBeenDamagedBy | Overloaded. |
| isAttachedToVehicle |  |
| isInArea |  |
| isInVehicle | Overloaded. |
| isSittingInVehicle | Overloaded. |
| isTouching | Overloaded. |
| LeaveGroup |  |
| LeaveVehicle |  |
| MakeProofTo |  |
| NoLongerNeeded |  |
| RandomizeOutfit |  |
| RemoveFakeNetworkName |  |
| SayAmbientSpeech |  |
| SetDefaultVoice |  |
| SetDefensiveArea |  |
| SetPathfinding |  |
| ShootAt |  |
| StartKillingSpree |  |
| WarpIntoVehicle |  |

## Quaternion Structure

For a list of all members of this type, see Quaternion Members.

[System.Object](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.object)

   [System.ValueType](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.valuetype)
      **GTA.Quaternion**

## Visual Basic

Public Structure Quaternion

## C#

public struct Quaternion

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

#### Requirements

**Namespace:**
GTA

**Assembly:** ScriptHookDotNet (in ScriptHookDotNet.dll)

#### See Also

Quaternion Members | GTA Namespace

---

<a id="gta-quaternionmembers"></a>

## Quaternion Members

Quaternion overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Identity | Gets the identity Quaternion (0, 0, 0, 1). |

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Adds two quaternions. |
| Divide | Divides a quaternion by another. |
| Dot | Calculates the dot product of two quaternions. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal. |
| FromRotation |  |
| Invert | Overloaded. Conjugates and renormalizes the quaternion. |
| Lerp | Performs a linear interpolation between two quaternion. |
| Multiply | Overloaded. Scales a quaternion by the given value. |
| Negate | Reverses the direction of a given quaternion. |
| Normalize | Overloaded. Converts the quaternion into a unit quaternion. |
| RotationAxis | Creates a quaternion given a rotation and an axis. |
| RotationMatrix | Creates a quaternion given a rotation matrix. |
| RotationYawPitchRoll | Creates a quaternion given a yaw, pitch, and roll value. |
| Subtract | Subtracts two quaternions. |

#### Public Static (Shared) Operators

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Addition Operator | Adds two quaternions. |
| Division Operator | Divides a quaternion by another. |
| Equality Operator | Tests for equality between two objects. |
| Inequality Operator | Tests for inequality between two objects. |
| Multiplication Operator | Overloaded. Multiplies a quaternion by another. |
| Subtraction Operator | Subtracts two quaternions. |
| Unary Negation Operator | Reverses the direction of a given quaternion. |

#### Public Instance Constructors

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quaternion | Overloaded. Initializes a new instance of the Quaternion class. |

#### Public Instance Fields

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| W | Gets or sets the W component of the quaternion. |
| X | Gets or sets the X component of the quaternion. |
| Y | Gets or sets the Y component of the quaternion. |
| Z | Gets or sets the Z component of the quaternion. |

#### Public Instance Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Angle | Gets the angle of the quaternion. |
| Axis | Gets the axis components of the quaternion. |

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Conjugate | Conjugates the quaternion. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Returns a value that indicates whether the current instance is equal to the specified object. |
| GetHashCode | Returns the hash code for this instance. |
| Invert | Overloaded. Conjugates and renormalizes the quaternion. |
| Length | Calculates the length of the quaternion. |
| Normalize | Overloaded. Converts the quaternion into a unit quaternion. |
| ToRotation |  |
| ToString | Overloaded. |

Performs a linear interpolation between two quaternion.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Function Lerp( \_
   ByVal *start* As GTA.Quaternion, \_
   ByVal *end* As GTA.Quaternion, \_
   ByVal *amount* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
) As GTA.Quaternion

## C#

public static GTA.Quaternion Lerp(
   GTA.Quaternion *start*,
   GTA.Quaternion *end*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *amount*
);

#### Parameters

*start*
:   Start quaternion.

*end*
:   End quaternion.

*amount*
:   Value between 0 and 1 indicating the weight of *end*.

#### Return Value

The linear interpolation of the two quaternions.

#### Remarks

This method performs the linear interpolation based on the following formula.

```
start + (end - start) * amount
```

Passing *amount* a value of 0 will cause *start* to be returned; a value of 1 will cause *end* to be returned.

#### See Also

Quaternion Class | GTA Namespace

<a id="gta-script-guid"></a>

## GUID Property

The GUID is used to uniquely idetify your script. With it you are able to communicate between scripts. DO NOT use the same GUID for multiple scripts! If you want to get a new GUID for your script, just google "generate guid" to find an online generator.

<a id="gta-script-keydown"></a>

## KeyDown Event

## Visual Basic

Public Event KeyDown As GTA.KeyEventHandler

## C#

public event GTA.KeyEventHandler KeyDown;

#### Event Data

The event handler receives an argument of type KeyEventArgs containing data related to this event. The following **KeyEventArgs** properties provide information specific to this event.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Alt | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Alt. |
| Control | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Control. |
| isModifier |  |
| isMouseButton |  |
| Key | Returns the key without any modifier information. |
| KeyWithModifiers | Returns the key combined with modifier (Shift, Control, Alt) information. |
| MouseButton |  |
| Shift | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Shift. |

#### See Also

Script Class | GTA Namespace

<a id="gta-settingsfilemembers"></a>

## SettingsFile Members

SettingsFile overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Open |  |

#### Public Instance Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Filename | The setting file has the same filename as the script, just with an 'ini' extension. |

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Clear |  |
| Dispose | Overloaded. |
| GetCategoryNames | Get the names of all categories. |
| GetValueBool | Overloaded. Get a boolean value with the given name from the settings file. INI values that return true: true, yes, on, 1. INI values that return false: false, no, off, 0. |
| GetValueFloat | Overloaded. Get a float value with the given name from the settings file. |
| GetValueInteger | Overloaded. Get an integer value with the given name from the settings file. |
| GetValueKey | Overloaded. Get a value with the given name from the settings file and convert is to a key. The key may be given as the key's name or as keycode. |
| GetValueModel | Overloaded. Get a value with the given name from the settings file and convert is to a model. The model may be given as modelname, integer or hex value. |
| GetValueNames | Overloaded. Get the names of all values in the default category. |
| GetValueString | Overloaded. Get a string value with the given name from the settings file. |
| GetValueVector3 | Overloaded. Get the value with the given name from the settings file and convert it to a Vector3. The 3 float values can be seperated by space or by a comma ',' . |
| Load | Load the settings file again. In is automatically loaded on script startup. |
| Save |  |
| SaveCopyTo |  |
| SetValue | Overloaded. |

#### Protected Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Dispose | Overloaded. |
| Finalize |  |

#### See Also

SettingsFile Class | GTA Namespace

<a id="gta-textalignment"></a>

## TextAlignment Enumeration

## Visual Basic

Public Enum TextAlignment

## C#

public enum TextAlignment

#### Members

| Member Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Bottom** | Align the text to the bottom. |
| **Right** | Align the text to the right. |
| **Left** | Align the text to the left. |
| **Top** | Align the text to the top. |
| **NoClip** | Don't clip the text. |
| **ExpandTabs** | Expand tab characters. |
| **SingleLine** | Force all text to a single line. |
| **WordBreak** | Allow word breaks. |
| **VerticalCenter** | Vertically align the text to the center. |
| **Center** | Align the text to the center. |

#### Requirements

**Namespace:**
GTA

**Assembly:** ScriptHookDotNet (in ScriptHookDotNet.dll)

#### See Also

GTA Namespace

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| ElapsedTime | Time elapsed since the last Start. Does not increase when the Timer is stopped. |
| Interval | The interval (in Milliseconds) at which the Tick event will be fired. The Tick event will not run if Interval is negative. Any Wait() in your Script will delay the next Tick until after the Wait is finished. |
| isRunning |  |

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Start | Starts the Timer and resets the ElapsedTime back to 0. The Tick event will fire each Interval if Interval is greater than or equal to 0. |
| Stop | Stops the Timer from firing Tick events and freezes the current value of ElapsedTime. |

<a id="gta-vector2members"></a>

## Vector2 Members

Vector2 overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Adds two vectors. |
| Clamp | Restricts a value to be within a specified range. |
| Distance | Calculates the distance between two vectors. |
| Divide | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Dot | Calculates the dot product of two vectors. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal. |
| Lerp | Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors. |
| Maximize | Returns a vector containing the largest components of the specified vectors. |
| Minimize | Returns a vector containing the smallest components of the specified vectors. |
| Modulate | Modulates a vector by another. |
| Multiply | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Negate | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |
| Normalize | Overloaded. Converts the vector into a unit vector. |
| Subtract | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Transform | Overloaded. Transforms a 2D vector by the given Quaternion rotation. |
| TransformCoordinate | Performs a coordinate transformation using the given Matrix. |
| TransformNormal | Performs a normal transformation using the given Matrix. |

|  |  |

| Multiplication Operator | Overloaded. Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Subtraction Operator | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Unary Negation Operator | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |

<a id="gta-vector2-lerp"></a>

## Lerp Method

Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Function Lerp( \_
   ByVal *start* As GTA.Vector2, \_
   ByVal *end* As GTA.Vector2, \_
   ByVal *amount* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
) As GTA.Vector2

## C#

public static GTA.Vector2 Lerp(
   GTA.Vector2 *start*,
   GTA.Vector2 *end*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *amount*
);

#### Parameters

*start*
:   Start vector.

*end*
:   End vector.

*amount*
:   Value between 0 and 1 indicating the weight of *end*.

#### Return Value

The linear interpolation of the two vectors.

#### Remarks

This method performs the linear interpolation based on the following formula.

```
start + (end - start) * amount
```

Passing *amount* a value of 0 will cause *start* to be returned; a value of 1 will cause *end* to be returned.

#### See Also

Vector2 Class | GTA Namespace

---

   [System.ValueType](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.valuetype)
      **GTA.Vector3**

## Visual Basic

Public Structure Vector3

## C#

public struct Vector3

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

#### Requirements

**Namespace:**
GTA

**Assembly:** ScriptHookDotNet (in ScriptHookDotNet.dll)

<a id="gta-vector3members"></a>

## Vector3 Members

Vector3 overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| RelativeBack | Returns the relative Back vector. (0,-1,0) |
| RelativeBottom | Returns the relative Bottom vector as used. (0,0,-1) |
| RelativeFront | Returns the relative Front vector. (0,1,0) |
| RelativeLeft | Returns the relative Left vector. (-1,0,0) |
| RelativeRight | Returns the relative Right vector. (1,0,0) |
| RelativeTop | Returns the relative Top vector. (0,0,1) |
| WorldDown | Returns the world Down vector. (0,0,-1) |
| WorldEast | Returns the world East vector. (1,0,0) |
| WorldNorth | Returns the world North vector. (0,1,0) |
| WorldSouth | Returns the world South vector. (0,-1,0) |
| WorldUp | Returns the world Up vector. (0,0,1) |
| WorldWest | Returns the world West vector. (-1,0,0) |
| Zero | Returns a null vector. (0,0,0) |

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Adds two vectors. |
| Clamp | Restricts a value to be within a specified range. |
| Cross | Calculates the cross product of two vectors. |
| Divide | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Dot | Calculates the dot product of two vectors. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal. |
| Lerp | Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors. |
| Maximize | Returns a vector containing the largest components of the specified vectors. |
| Minimize | Returns a vector containing the smallest components of the specified vectors. |
| Modulate | Modulates a vector by another. |
| Multiply | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Negate | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |
| Normalize | Overloaded. Converts the vector into a unit vector. |
| Project | Projects a 3D vector from object space into screen space. |
| RandomXY |  |
| RandomXYZ |  |
| Reflect | Returns the reflection of a vector off a surface that has the specified normal. |
| Subtract | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Transform | Overloaded. Transforms a 3D vector by the given Quaternion rotation. |
| TransformCoordinate | Performs a coordinate transformation using the given Matrix. |
| TransformNormal | Performs a normal transformation using the given Matrix. |
| Unproject | Projects a 3D vector from screen space into object space. |

#### Public Static (Shared) Operators

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Addition Operator | Adds two vectors. |
| Division Operator | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Equality Operator | Tests for equality between two objects. |
| Inequality Operator | Tests for inequality between two objects. |
| Multiplication Operator | Overloaded. Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Subtraction Operator | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Unary Negation Operator | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Vector3 | Overloaded. Initializes a new instance of the Vector3 class. |

| Y | Gets or sets the Y component of the vector. |

<a id="gta-vector3-lerp"></a>

## Lerp Method

Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Function Lerp( \_
   ByVal *start* As GTA.Vector3, \_
   ByVal *end* As GTA.Vector3, \_
   ByVal *amount* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
) As GTA.Vector3

## C#

public static GTA.Vector3 Lerp(
   GTA.Vector3 *start*,
   GTA.Vector3 *end*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *amount*
);

#### Parameters

*start*
:   Start vector.

*end*
:   End vector.

*amount*
:   Value between 0 and 1 indicating the weight of *end*.

#### Return Value

The linear interpolation of the two vectors.

#### Remarks

This method performs the linear interpolation based on the following formula.

```
start + (end - start) * amount
```

Passing *amount* a value of 0 will cause *start* to be returned; a value of 1 will cause *end* to be returned.

#### See Also

Vector3 Class | GTA Namespace

---

) As GTA.Vector3

## C#

   GTA.Vector3 *vector*,
   [int](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32) *x*,
   [int](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.int32) *y*,

);

*height*
:   The height of the viewport.

*minZ*
:   The minimum depth of the viewport.

*maxZ*
:   The maximum depth of the viewport.

:   The combined world-view-projection matrix.

<a id="gta-vector3operators"></a>

## Operators

The operators of the **Vector3** structure are listed below. For a complete list of **Vector3** structure members, see the Vector3 Members topic.

#### Public Static (Shared) Operators

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Addition Operator | Adds two vectors. |
| Division Operator | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Equality Operator | Tests for equality between two objects. |
| Inequality Operator | Tests for inequality between two objects. |
| Multiplication Operator | Overloaded. Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Subtraction Operator | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Unary Negation Operator | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |

#### See Also

Vector3 Class | Vector3 Members | GTA Namespace

---

<a id="gta-vector3-op-addition"></a>

## Addition Operator

Adds two vectors.

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

<a id="gta-vector4members"></a>

## Vector4 Members

Vector4 overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Adds two vectors. |
| Clamp | Restricts a value to be within a specified range. |
| Distance | Calculates the distance between two vectors. |
| Divide | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Dot | Calculates the dot product of two vectors. |
| Equals | Overloaded. Determines whether the specified object instances are considered equal. |
| Lerp | Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors. |
| Maximize | Returns a vector containing the largest components of the specified vectors. |
| Minimize | Returns a vector containing the smallest components of the specified vectors. |
| Modulate | Modulates a vector by another. |
| Multiply | Scales a vector by the given value. |
| Negate | Reverses the direction of a given vector. |
| Normalize | Overloaded. Converts the vector into a unit vector. |
| Subtract | Subtracts two vectors. |
| Transform | Overloaded. Transforms a 4D vector by the given Quaternion rotation. |

<a id="gta-vector4-lerp"></a>

## Lerp Method

Performs a linear interpolation between two vectors.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Function Lerp( \_
   ByVal *start* As GTA.Vector4, \_
   ByVal *end* As GTA.Vector4, \_
   ByVal *amount* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
) As GTA.Vector4

## C#

public static GTA.Vector4 Lerp(
   GTA.Vector4 *start*,
   GTA.Vector4 *end*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *amount*
);

#### Parameters

*start*
:   Start vector.

*end*
:   End vector.

*amount*
:   Value between 0 and 1 indicating the weight of *end*.

#### Return Value

The linear interpolation of the two vectors.

#### Remarks

This method performs the linear interpolation based on the following formula.

```
start + (end - start) * amount
```

Passing *amount* a value of 0 will cause *start* to be returned; a value of 1 will cause *end* to be returned.

#### See Also

Vector4 Class | GTA Namespace

---

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

| Color | Gets or sets the base color of the vehicle. |
| CurrentRoom | Gets or sets the interior room the vehicle currently is at. It needs to be set correctly for the vehicle to be visible inside the room. |

| Dirtyness |  |
| DoorLock |  |

Gets or sets the rotation of the vehicle. X is pitch, Y is roll, Z is yaw. Can be slightly off. Use RotationQuaternion if it should be perfectly accurate.

<a id="gta-vehiclemethods"></a>

## Methods

The methods of the **Vehicle** class are listed below. For a complete list of **Vehicle** class members, see the Vehicle Members topic.

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| ApplyForce | Overloaded. Applies a force given in world coordinates to the vehicle. |
| ApplyForceRelative | Overloaded. Applies a force that is relative to the current orientation of the vehicle. Directions: positive X is right, positive Y is front, positive Z is Top |
| AttachBlip |  |
| BurstTire |  |
| CloseAllDoors |  |
| CreatePedOnSeat | Overloaded. |
| Delete |  |
| Door |  |
| Equals (inherited from **HandleObject**) | Overloaded. |
| EveryoneLeaveVehicle |  |
| Exists (inherited from **object**) | Returns whether the object still exists in game. Other calls to non-existing objects will result in an error. This function is FAST and is better checked too much rather than too less. |
| Explode |  |
| Extras |  |
| FixTire |  |
| GetFreePassengerSeat |  |
| GetFreeSeat |  |
| GetHashCode (inherited from **HandleObject**) |  |
| GetOffset | Returns the given position as directional offset using the following directions: positive X is right, positive Y is in front, positive Z is on Top |
| GetOffsetPosition | Returns the given offset in world coordinates. Directions: positive X is right, positive Y is front, positive Z is Top |
| GetPedOnSeat |  |
| isSeatFree |  |
| IsTireBurst |  |
| isTouching |  |
| MakeProofTo |  |
| NoLongerNeeded |  |
| PassengersLeaveVehicle | Overloaded. |
| PlaceOnGroundProperly |  |
| PlaceOnNextStreetProperly |  |
| Repair |  |
| SoundHorn |  |
| Wash |  |

#### See Also

Vehicle Class | GTA Namespace

NotInheritable Public Class World

public sealed class World

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

| GetStreetName |  |
| GetVehicles | Overloaded. Returns all vehicles in the given area. Slow, but very reliable! Also includes player and mission vehicles! |

<a id="gta-world-setdoorstate"></a>

## SetDoorState Method

Sets the state of the closest door with the given model.

## Visual Basic

Public Shared Sub SetDoorState( \_
   ByVal *DoorModel* As GTA.Model, \_
   ByVal *NearbyPosition* As GTA.Vector3, \_
   ByVal *Locked* As [Boolean](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.boolean), \_
   ByVal *Angle* As [Single](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) \_
)

## C#

public static [void](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.void) SetDoorState(
   GTA.Model *DoorModel*,
   GTA.Vector3 *NearbyPosition*,
   [bool](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.boolean) *Locked*,
   [float](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.single) *Angle*
);

#### Parameters

*DoorModel*
:   Model of the nearby door to open.

*NearbyPosition*
:   A position close to the door.

*Locked*
:   The door cannot be moved when locked.

*Angle*
:   The current door angle. 0 is closed, 1 is open, -1 is open to the other direction.

#### See Also

World Class | GTA Namespace

---

MustInherit Public Class BaseCollection<>

public abstract class BaseCollection<> **: [System.Collections.Generic.IList<>](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.generic.ilist<>), [System.Collections.Generic.ICollection<>](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.generic.icollection<>), [System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<>](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.generic.ienumerable<>), [System.Collections.IEnumerable](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.ienumerable)**

#### Thread Safety

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

**Namespace:**
GTA.base

safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

<a id="gta-euphoria-customhelper-start-overloads"></a>

## Start Method

Start the Euphoria animation. It will run up to a maximum of 65 seconds, or until the first previous Euphoria animation ends. Previous Euphoria animations will NOT be disabled.

#### Overload List

Start the Euphoria animation. It will run up to a maximum of 65 seconds, or until the first previous Euphoria animation ends. Previous Euphoria animations will NOT be disabled.

> public void Start();

Start the Euphoria animation. Maximum duration is 65.535 milliseconds. Calling this will disable previous Euphoria animations.

> public void Start(int);

thread-safe.

<a id="gta-forms-control-keydown"></a>

## KeyDown Event

## Visual Basic

Public Event KeyDown As GTA.KeyEventHandler

## C#

public event GTA.KeyEventHandler KeyDown;

#### Event Data

The event handler receives an argument of type KeyEventArgs containing data related to this event. The following **KeyEventArgs** properties provide information specific to this event.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Alt | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Alt. |
| Control | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Control. |
| isModifier |  |
| isMouseButton |  |
| Key | Returns the key without any modifier information. |
| KeyWithModifiers | Returns the key combined with modifier (Shift, Control, Alt) information. |
| MouseButton |  |
| Shift | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Shift. |

#### See Also

Control Class | GTA.Forms Namespace

---

<a id="gta-forms-keybox-selectedkeychanged"></a>

## SelectedKeyChanged Event

## Visual Basic

Public Event SelectedKeyChanged As GTA.KeyEventHandler

## C#

public event GTA.KeyEventHandler SelectedKeyChanged;

#### Event Data

The event handler receives an argument of type KeyEventArgs containing data related to this event. The following **KeyEventArgs** properties provide information specific to this event.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Alt | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Alt. |
| Control | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Control. |
| isModifier |  |
| isMouseButton |  |
| Key | Returns the key without any modifier information. |
| KeyWithModifiers | Returns the key combined with modifier (Shift, Control, Alt) information. |
| MouseButton |  |
| Shift | Returns if the key was pressed in combination with Shift. |

#### See Also

Keybox Class | GTA.Forms Namespace

---

Public static (**Shared** in Visual Basic) members of this type are
safe for multithreaded operations. Instance members are **not** guaranteed to be
thread-safe.

<a id="gta-value-pedcomponentmembers"></a>

## PedComponent Members

PedComponent overview

#### Public Static (Shared) Operators and Type Conversions

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Equality Operator | Overloaded. |
| Inequality Operator | Overloaded. |
| Implicit PedComponent to PedComponent Conversion |  |

#### Public Instance Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| AvailableModels | Returns the number of available models for the component. |
| AvailableTextures | Returns the number of available textures for the currently selected component model. |
| ModelIndex | Gets or sets the index of the model for this component. Changing it will reset the TextureIndex to 0! (Value range: 0 to AvailableModels-1) |
| TextureIndex | Gets or sets the index of the texture for the currently selected component model. (Value range: 0 to AvailableTextures-1) |

#### Public Instance Methods

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Change | Changes ModelIndex and TextureIndex at the same time. May throw an ArgumentOutOfRangeException when a given index is invalid. |
| ChangeIfValid | Changes ModelIndex and TextureIndex at the same time. Will do nothing when a given index is invalid. |
| isValid | Returns whether or not the given model and texture index combination is valid. |
| SetToDefault |  |

#### See Also

PedComponent Class | GTA.value Namespace
